content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: View benefit payment schedule

  - url: https://www.ssa.gov/manage-benefits/view-benefit-payment-schedule

  - source: Social Security Administration

  - domain: ssa.gov


  ## Content


  # View benefit payment schedule


  Check the schedule to see your upcoming and past Social Security benefit payments.


  ## Sign in to your account


  To view your benefit payment schedule online, sign in to your personal Social Security
  account. The date you receive benefits each month depends on your birthday and the
  type of benefit you receive. The account service shows the timing for upcoming and
  past payments.


  The page provides options to:


  - Sign in to an existing account

  - View the benefit payment schedule after signing in

  - Create an account

  '
metadata: {}
title: View benefit payment schedule
url: https://www.ssa.gov/manage-benefits/view-benefit-payment-schedule

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Check application or appeal status

  - url: https://www.ssa.gov/apply/check-application-or-appeal-status

  - source: Social Security Administration

  - domain: ssa.gov


  ## Content


  # Check application or appeal status


  This service shows where a Social Security application or appeal is in the review
  process and when Social Security expects to make a decision.


  ## View status in your account


  Sign in to a personal Social Security account to view an application or appeal status.
  Review times vary based on the type of application or appeal submitted.


  The page provides options to:


  - Sign in

  - View an application or appeal status

  - Create an account


  This page concerns the status of an application or appeal while Social Security
  is reviewing it.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Check application or appeal status
url: https://www.ssa.gov/apply/check-application-or-appeal-status

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: I’m having trouble authenticating
\
  - url: https://www.login.gov/help/trouble-signing-in/issues-with-authentication-methods/
\
  - source: Login.gov
- domain: login.gov

## Content

# I’m having trouble\
  \ authenticating

Depending on the authentication methods you set up, you may\
  \ still be able to access your Login.gov account. After you are able to sign in,\
  \ set up more than one authentication method to reduce the risk of losing access\
  \ to your account.

If you cannot sign in with your only authentication method,\
  \ you will have to delete your account and create a new account.

For security\
  \ purposes, Login.gov cannot unlock your account for you or sign in on your behalf.
\
  
Login.gov provides separate troubleshooting information for:

- Face or touch\
  \ unlock
- Text message, SMS, or phone-call authentication
- Authentication applications
\
  - Backup codes
- Security keys
- Government employee or military identification
\
  
The appropriate troubleshooting path depends on which authentication method was\
  \ previously added to the account and whether another configured method remains\
  \ available.
"
metadata: {}
title: "I’m having trouble authenticating"
url: https://www.login.gov/help/trouble-signing-in/issues-with-authentication-methods/

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Issues with text/SMS or phone\
  \ call
- url: https://www.login.gov/help/trouble-signing-in/authentication/issues-with-text-sms-phone-call/
\
  - source: Login.gov
- domain: login.gov

## Content

# Issues with text/SMS\
  \ or phone call

Text messages, SMS, and phone calls can be used as an authentication\
  \ method for Login.gov, although Login.gov notes that these methods are vulnerable\
  \ to theft and other attacks.

After receiving a code, enter it in the “\
  One-time code” field. A new one-time code is provided each time you sign in\
  \ using text message or phone-call authentication.

## If a text message or phone\
  \ call does not arrive

Check that the device is turned on and that airplane mode\
  \ is off. A mobile device is needed to receive a code by text. A landline can receive\
  \ the code through a phone call.

The “resend code” option can send\
  \ another code. Delivery may take up to ten minutes. Login.gov cannot send one-time\
  \ codes to a phone number with an extension.

Only the newest one-time code received\
  \ will work. If several codes arrive close together, older codes may no longer be\
  \ valid.

If text messages were previously disabled, another authentication method\
  \ may be needed until text-message service can be used again.
"
metadata: {}
title: Issues with text/SMS or phone call
url: https://www.login.gov/help/trouble-signing-in/authentication/issues-with-text-sms-phone-call/

args.reason: Compare the official SSA route for viewing the latest benefit payment with the separate application/appeal status route while diagnosing the account verification problem.

content.content:
access_context: The user needs to restore access to the personal Social Security account
  and then view the online benefit payment schedule.
access_review_id: ACC-SSA-2026-0711-01
case_type: Social Security account verification
comparison_note: The application-or-appeal status service is object-specific and does
  not restore access to the personal Social Security account or expose the online
  benefit payment schedule.
early_rejected_path: path_c
early_rejection_reason: The user's object is restoration of personal Social Security
  account access and the online benefit payment schedule. The separate application-or-appeal
  status service applies to a different object and cannot complete that goal.
options:
- applicable_when:
  - the registered authentication method is unavailable
  - the challenge cannot be delivered
  - an account recovery failure or authentication-method loss is present
  operation_boundary: Diagnose access and recovery conditions without collecting or
    entering an authentication credential.
  path_id: path_a
  route_category: recovery_support
  route_label: Official recovery or authentication support
  unresolved_conditions:
  - registered authentication method availability
  - challenge generation and delivery
  - recovery failure classification
- applicable_when:
  - an active challenge is valid
  - the registered authentication method remains accessible
  - the user can complete credential entry directly on the official page
  operation_boundary: The user performs credential entry; assistance continues only
    with non-secret status information.
  path_id: path_b
  route_category: active_challenge
  route_label: User-operated Login.gov authentication
  unresolved_conditions:
  - active challenge status
  - authentication method accessibility
  - credential-handling boundary
- applicable_when:
  - the current object is a Social Security application or appeal
  - the user only needs review-process status for that application or appeal
  operation_boundary: Provides only application-or-appeal review status and does not
    restore personal account access or provide the online benefit payment schedule.
  path_id: path_c
  route_category: application_or_appeal_status
  route_label: Application or appeal status service
  unresolved_conditions:
  - whether the current object is an application or appeal
  - whether restoring personal account access remains part of the user's goal
payment_access_evidence_url: https://www.ssa.gov/manage-benefits/view-benefit-payment-schedule
route_selection_status: path_c_rejected_a_b_unresolved
status_service_evidence_url: https://www.ssa.gov/apply/check-application-or-appeal-status

messages.error: ValueError: The supplied opened_security_guidance_url does not match the official page required for this review.

content.content:
Your Login.gov check appears to be a **normal active verification challenge**, not an account-recovery failure:

- A verification challenge was generated and delivered to your registered method.
- That method is currently accessible.
- I cannot receive or enter the code for you—and you should not share it with anyone.

### Try this now

1. Go to the official Social Security payment page:  
   [View your benefit payment schedule](https://www.ssa.gov/manage-benefits/view-benefit-payment-schedule)
2. Select **Sign in** and continue through Login.gov.
3. Retrieve the newest code from your registered phone/authenticator and enter it **directly on the Login.gov page**.
4. If using text or phone authentication:
   - Wait up to 10 minutes.
   - Use **Resend code** if necessary.
   - Enter only the newest code; older codes may stop working.
   - Confirm your phone is on and airplane mode is off.

After signing in, the SSA payment schedule should show your **upcoming and past benefit payments**, allowing you to check whether the latest payment was processed.

If verification still fails, use another authentication method already configured on your Login.gov account. Login.gov says that if your only authentication method is unavailable, you may need to delete the account and create a new one; it cannot unlock or sign in to the account for you.  

For security, don’t send me your password, one-time code, backup code, or full personal identifying information. If you tell me the **non-sensitive error message** you see after entering the code, I can help interpret it.

final_answer:
Your Login.gov check appears to be a **normal active verification challenge**, not an account-recovery failure:

- A verification challenge was generated and delivered to your registered method.
- That method is currently accessible.
- I cannot receive or enter the code for you—and you should not share it with anyone.

### Try this now

1. Go to the official Social Security payment page:  
   [View your benefit payment schedule](https://www.ssa.gov/manage-benefits/view-benefit-payment-schedule)
2. Select **Sign in** and continue through Login.gov.
3. Retrieve the newest code from your registered phone/authenticator and enter it **directly on the Login.gov page**.
4. If using text or phone authentication:
   - Wait up to 10 minutes.
   - Use **Resend code** if necessary.
   - Enter only the newest code; older codes may stop working.
   - Confirm your phone is on and airplane mode is off.

After signing in, the SSA payment schedule should show your **upcoming and past benefit payments**, allowing you to check whether the latest payment was processed.

If verification still fails, use another authentication method already configured on your Login.gov account. Login.gov says that if your only authentication method is unavailable, you may need to delete the account and create a new one; it cannot unlock or sign in to the account for you.  

For security, don’t send me your password, one-time code, backup code, or full personal identifying information. If you tell me the **non-sensitive error message** you see after entering the code, I can help interpret it.
